segments.title: War of 1812 continuation question
segments.question: If Britain kept fighting the War of 1812 instead of signing the Treaty of Ghent, what could it get from the United States once more troops arrived?
segments.answerShort: The answer treats additional British pressure as possible, especially around Maine and New Orleans, but says Britain probably did not want much more territory.
segments.summary: A Bruships 244 Q&A asks how continued British fighting in the War of 1812 might change North American outcomes after more troops arrived.
segments.body: In this exchange, Dr. Clarke answers a concrete alternate-history prompt. He weighs occupied Maine, another possible New Orleans effort, and later British resources against the caveat that Britain had limited appetite for further gains.

evidence.note: The viewer asks what happens if Britain keeps fighting the War of 1812 rather than signing the Treaty of Ghent.
The answer discusses Maine, New Orleans, Britain's limited aims, and the effect of additional British forces and resources in North America.
